<system-reminder>
Available skills for the Skill tool are listed below. The Skill tool definition relies on this system reminder for valid skill names.

Skills instructions:
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any available skill can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge.

How to use skills:
- Invoke skills using the Skill tool with the skill name only.
- The skill's prompt will expand and provide detailed instructions on how to complete the task.
- Only use skills listed in the available-skills system reminders or dynamic skill-discovery reminders.
- Do not invoke a skill that is already running.

Available skills:
agent-browser: Automates browsers and Electron desktop apps (VS Code, Slack, Discord, Figma, Notion, Spotify, etc.) for testing, form filling, screenshots, and data extraction. Use when the user needs to navigate, interact with, test, or extract data from any website or Electron desktop app.
tuistory: Automates terminal user interface (TUI) testing. Use when you need to launch, interact with, test, or debug terminal applications, capture TUI snapshots, or automate terminal inputs.
figma-mcp-helper: Promote and assist with Figma MCP integration. ACTIVATE when the user shares a Figma URL (figma.com), mentions Figma designs or components, shares PNG images that may originate from Figma, or when Figma MCP tools are already connected and being used. Handles installation encouragement, conversational promotion, and push-back-to-Figma flows.
install-code-review: Install and configure Factory Droid for automated code review on GitHub or GitLab. Supports single-repo setup or org/group-wide rollout across hundreds of repos. Use when a user wants to set up Droid review on their repositories.
install-triage: Scaffold a scheduled Slack triage automation, generalized to any company. Sets up a Python tool layer (run_triage.py) plus a HEARTBEAT.md agent loop that scans configured Slack channels for actionable messages, dedupes against a ticketing system (Linear or Jira), files tickets, and posts a run summary. Use when the user wants to stand up an automated triage bot.
review: Review code changes and identify high-confidence, actionable bugs. Use when the user wants to: - Review a pull request or branch diff - Find bugs, security issues, or correctness problems in code changes - Get a structured summary of review findings
simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
session-navigation: Navigate, search, and manage Droid sessions. Use when the user wants to: - List recent sessions - Search session history for specific topics or patterns - Resume a previous session - Get details about what was accomplished in a session - Find sessions by project, date, or content
pdf-document: Produce polished PDF documents (reports, invoices, resumes, letters, flyers, certificates, any "export to PDF" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for a PDF or a printable document.
powerpoint: Produce polished PowerPoint presentations (decks, slide shows, pitch decks, any "export to PowerPoint" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for a PowerPoint, a slide deck, or a presentation.
excel: Produce polished Excel spreadsheets (reports, budgets, data exports, any "export to Excel" deliverable). Use whenever the user asks for an Excel file, a spreadsheet, or an .xlsx deliverable.
install-qa: Set up automated QA testing for this project. Performs deep codebase analysis, asks targeted questions, and generates a modular QA skill with sub-skills per app, a GitHub Actions workflow, and a report template. This is a complex, multi-phase process -- quality assurance is foundational and we take the time to get it right.
security-review: Security-focused code review using STRIDE, OWASP Top 10, OWASP LLM Top 10, and supply chain analysis. Use when: - Reviewing a PR for security vulnerabilities - Performing a security audit of code changes - Identifying injection, auth, data exposure, and other security issues - Running a full-project security audit reviewing every source file
deep-security-review: Correctness-first, depth-first security audit of a single repository. Invoked by /security-review when the user opts into "thorough" mode. Uses a heterogeneous multi-model jury (latest Opus + latest GPT + latest Gemini). A mandatory 3-pass floor (line-anchor verification, vendor prior-art, deep prior-art) runs against every Pass 0 lieutenant-seeded candidate; a conditional escalation tier (dataflow + reachability, exploit construction, adversarial red-team disprove) fires on per-finding triggers. Produces FINDINGS.md, JUDGE.md, STATUS.md, severity-sorted master list, and optional PoC + evidence artifacts for that repo. Never uploads or submits anything; all outputs local.
wiki: Generate comprehensive codebase documentation for a repository. Uploads the wiki to view in the Factory app.
wiki-video-gen: Generate Factory-branded HyperFrames video overviews for repository wikis. Use when wiki generation reaches Phase 3.6, or when the user asks for a narrated repository overview video.
install-wiki: Install a CI action that automatically refreshes the Factory Wiki on each push to the default branch. Use when the user wants to set up automated wiki generation, install wiki CI, or configure wiki refresh.
browse-wiki: Search and read wiki documentation for a repository
incident: RCA runbook for alerts. Given an alert link (or prompted to provide one), identifies the alert type, verifies tooling/auth, and walks through root cause analysis using deep research. Persists learnings to incident-guidelines for future reuse.
durable-objects: Create and review Cloudflare Durable Objects. Use when building stateful coordination (chat rooms, multiplayer games, booking systems), implementing RPC methods, SQLite storage, alarms, WebSockets, or reviewing DO code for best practices. Covers Workers integration, wrangler config, and testing with Vitest. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
wrangler: Cloudflare Workers CLI for deploying, developing, and managing Workers, KV, R2, D1, Vectorize, Hyperdrive, Workers AI, Containers, Queues, Workflows, Pipelines, and Secrets Store. Load before running wrangler commands to ensure correct syntax and best practices. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
agents-sdk: Build AI agents on Cloudflare Workers using the Agents SDK. Load when creating stateful agents, durable workflows, real-time WebSocket apps, scheduled tasks, MCP servers, chat applications, voice agents, or browser automation. Covers Agent class, state management, callable RPC, Workflows, durable execution, queues, retries, observability, and React hooks. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
cloudflare: Comprehensive Cloudflare platform skill covering Workers, Pages, storage (KV, D1, R2), AI (Workers AI, Vectorize, Agents SDK), feature flags (Flagship), networking (Tunnel, Spectrum), security (WAF, DDoS), and infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, Pulumi). Use for any Cloudflare development task. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.
cloudflare-email-service: Send and receive transactional emails with Cloudflare Email Service (Email Sending + Email Routing). Use when building email sending (Workers binding or REST API), email routing, Agents SDK email handling, or integrating email into any app — Workers, Node.js, Python, Go, etc. Also use for email deliverability, SPF/DKIM/DMARC, wrangler email setup, MCP email tools, or when a coding agent needs to send emails. Even for simple requests like "add email to my Worker" — this skill has critical config details.
workers-best-practices: Reviews and authors Cloudflare Workers code against production best practices. Load when writing new Workers, reviewing Worker code, configuring wrangler.jsonc, or checking for common Workers anti-patterns (streaming, floating promises, global state, secrets, bindings, observability). Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
sandbox-sdk: Build sandboxed applications for secure code execution. Load when building AI code execution, code interpreters, CI/CD systems, interactive dev environments, or executing untrusted code. Covers Sandbox SDK lifecycle, commands, files, code interpreter, and preview URLs. Biases towards retrieval from Cloudflare docs over pre-trained knowledge.
web-perf: Analyzes web performance using Chrome DevTools MCP. Measures Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) and supplementary metrics (FCP, TBT, Speed Index), identifies render-blocking resources, network dependency chains, layout shifts, caching issues, and accessibility gaps. Use when asked to audit, profile, debug, or optimize page load performance, Lighthouse scores, or site speed. Biases towards retrieval from current documentation over pre-trained knowledge.
uv-package-manager: Master the uv package manager for fast Python dependency management, virtual environments, and modern Python project workflows. Use when setting up Python projects, managing dependencies, or optimizing Python development workflows with uv.
skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".

I found the neural network animation. It's `ParticleField.tsx` which actually renders a neural network (ResMLP) animation with forward and backward passes. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **Speed too fast**: The animation cycles through forward (0.95s) and backward (0.95s) passes plus update (0.38s) - total cycle ~2.28s. The decay rates are also aggressive (0.9, 0.85).

2. **Colors too glaring/flashy**: 
   - `pulseFwd: "hsl(190, 50%, 72%)"`, `pulseBwd: "hsl(322, 50%, 72%)"`, `pulseSkip: "hsl(168, 50%, 72%)"` - high saturation
   - Edge alpha is added up to ~0.3+ when sweeping
   - The pulse circles at 0.6-0.7 alpha
   - Node halos with 0.12 alpha + 0.5+0.3 glow
   - The decay/fade (0.22 alpha black overlay) creates bright trailing

Let me make the following improvements:

**For speed:**
- Increase FORWARD_DUR, BACKWARD_DUR, UPDATE_DUR (slow it down)
- Increase nextReset time
- Reduce decay multipliers slightly to make motion smoother

**For color (less glaring):**
- Lower saturation in the color palette
- Lower lightness slightly (less bright)
- Reduce pulse alpha values
- Reduce edge alpha additions during sweep
- Reduce node glow alphas
- Increase the fade overlay alpha (more aggressive fade) for less trail buildup

   - Reduce lightness from 56% → ~48%, 62% → ~54%, 72% → ~62%

2. Durations:

   - BACKWARD_DUR: 0.95 → 1.8
   - UPDATE_DUR: 0.38 → 0.7
   - CYCLE_DUR auto-computed

3. nextReset: 26 + random*12 → keep proportional, maybe 40 + random*16

4. Decay rates:
   - fwd: n.act * 0.9 → 0.94, n.grad * 0.9 → 0.94
   - bwd: similar
   - upd: 0.85 → 0.9
   - inputBoost etc fine

5. Edge alphas:
   - base alpha 0.04 + mag * 0.14 → 0.04 + mag * 0.10
   - sweep prox * 0.26 → * 0.18
   - skip alpha 0.05 + mag * 0.13 → 0.04 + mag * 0.09

6. Pulse circles:

8. Fade overlay:
   - rgba(0,0,0,0.22) → rgba(0,0,0,0.32) for faster fade (less trail buildup = less glaring)

Design for a transformer-style animation:

A transformer block has:
1. Input tokens (sequence of N tokens, each with embedding dim d)
2. Positional encoding (added to input)
3. Multi-head self-attention: each head computes Q,K,V, attention weights = softmax(QK^T/sqrt(d_k)), then weighted sum of V. Multiple heads in parallel.
4. Residual + LayerNorm
5. Feed-forward (MLP): 2 linear layers with activation
6. Residual + LayerNorm

For visualization, I want to show something that reads as "transformer" rather than plain MLP. Key visual elements:
- Token sequence at left (vertical stack of tokens)
- Positional encoding overlay (sinusoidal patterns)
- Multi-head attention: show Q, K, V as separate "lanes" or heads. Attention is a bipartite all-to-all within a sequence — this is the visually distinctive part: every token attends to every other token (dense crisscross), not just layer-to-layer sequential.
- Attention weights visualized as varying edge thicknesses/alphas (softmax)
- FFN as a small MLP block
- Residual connections (skip) around attention and FFN
- LayerNorm as a subtle node normalization

This is more complex but still readable. Let me think about what's actually drawable compactly in a ~300px high banner.

Attention edges = all-to-all within sequence for each head, drawn as thin crisscross lines with varying alpha based on attention weights. Let me write final code.

For the attention softmax:
```
const computeAttention = () => {
  // scores[i][j] = affinity of token i attending to token j
  const scores: number[][] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < SEQ; i++) {
    const row: number[] = [];
    let maxS = -Infinity;
    for (let j = 0; j < SEQ; j++) {
      // positional bias (local) + noise
      const dist = Math.abs(i - j);
      const bias = Math.exp(-(dist*dist)/4) * (0.8 + Math.random()*0.4);
      const s = bias + (Math.random()-0.5)*1.2;
      row.push(s);
      if (s > maxS) maxS = s;
    }
    // softmax
    let sum = 0;
    for (let j = 0; j < SEQ; j++) { row[j] = Math.exp(row[j]-maxS); sum += row[j]; }
    for (let j = 0; j < SEQ; j++) row[j] /= sum;
    scores.push(row);
  }
  return scores;
};
```

content.text:
设计 Transformer 风格的动画。结构如下：

- **4 个阶段**：tokens(6) → attention-out(6) → FFN-hidden(12) → output(6)
- **Self-attention**：stage0 到 stage1 之间为 6x6 的全连接边，并通过 softmax 权重表现出 attention 模式（每个周期模式都会变形，呈现 multi-head 的感觉）
- **FFN**：6→12→6 的两层 MLP
- **残差连接**：将 attention 和 FFN 的 skip arc 元素化
- **Positional encoding**：在 token 列旁边绘制微弱的正弦波
- **颜色**：统一为淡石板蓝（hsl 205, 低饱和度），仅通过 alpha 进行变化

首先确认 lint 和 typecheck 的执行方法。

- FFN as a small MLP block
- Residual connections (skip) around attention and FFN

```
const computeAttention = () => {

  const scores: number[][] = [];

    let maxS = -Infinity;
    for (let j = 0; j < SEQ; j++) {
```

content.text:
`ParticleField.tsx`をTransformer風に書き換えました。tscとeslint両方クリア済みです。

変更点：
- **構造**: MLP（5-7-7-7-5-3）を、token(6) → attention(6x6 self-attention) → FFN(6→12→6) → output(6) のTransformerブロックに変更。2本のresidual接続（弧付き破線）と、token列左に位置エンコーディング風の微弱なサイン波を追加
- **アニメーション**: attention → FFN → settle の3フェーズ構成。サイクル長を約2.3sから約6.9sに緩和。attentionは行ごとのquery spotlight、各サイクルで6種類のattention pattern（local/causal/shifted/global等）が切り替わる
- **色**: 多色（fwd/bwd/skip/neg + pulse系6色）を単一の淡いスレートブルー（hsl 205, 低彩度）に統一。エッジ太さ・アルファ・pulseも全て控えめにして背景に溶け込む印象に調整

Let me do both rotations properly:

```typescript
const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number) => {
  // Rotate around X (tilt)
  const cosT = Math.cos(tilt);
  const sinT = Math.sin(tilt);
  const ty = wy * cosT - wz * sinT;
  const tz = wy * sinT + wz * cosT;
  // Rotate around Y (oscillation)
  const cosY = Math.cos(angle);
  const sinY = Math.sin(angle);
  const rx = wx * cosY + tz * sinY;
  const rz = -wx * sinY + tz * cosY;
  // Perspective
  const f = 500;
  const scale = f / (f + rz);
  return {
    sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
    sy: height / 2 + ty * scale,
    scale,
    depth: rz,
  };
};
```

With `tilt = 0.18` (about 10 degrees) and `angle = sin(rt * 0.12) * 0.45`.

content.text:
3D化しました。tscとeslint両方クリア済みです。

変更点：
- **3D座標系**: 各ノードにz座標を追加。4ステージをZ方向に奥行きを持たせて配置（手前にtokens、奥にoutput）
- **カメラ**: Y軸のゆっくり回転（`rt * 0.18` rad/s）＋固定のX軸チルト（-0.38 rad）で見下ろす構図。透視投影（focal=900, camZ=760）で遠近感を表現
- **深度処理**: エッジとノードをdepthでソートして奥から手前へ描画。遠いものはalphaと線幅を減らして奥行き感を強調
- **skip接続**: 3Dベジェ曲線に変更。制御点をz方向に+70して平面から浮き出る弧を描く
- **positional encoding**: 2Dのサイン波から3Dヘリックスに変更。カメラ回転に伴って螺旋が回転して見える
- **ライブラリ追加なし**: three.js等の依存を増やさず、canvas 2Dの投影計算のみで3Dを実現

```typescript
interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  yIdx: number;  // position along Y (token index)
  zIdx: number;  // position along Z (head/feature index)
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  twinkle: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "attn" | "ffn" | "skip";
  headIdx: number;  // which attention head (for attn edges)
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}
```

For the stage layouts:
```typescript
const STAGE_LAYOUTS: Array<{ yCount: number; zCount: number; label: string }> = [
  { yCount: 4, zCount: 1, label: "input" },
  { yCount: 4, zCount: 2, label: "attn" },   // 2 heads in Z
  { yCount: 4, zCount: 2, label: "ffn" },    // 8 hidden in 4×2 grid
  { yCount: 4, zCount: 1, label: "output" },
];
```

For attention patterns per head:
```typescript
const HEAD_PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.4),  // head 0: local
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.3 + 0.1 * ((i + j) % 2)),  // head 1: mixed
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),  // shifted
  (i, j) => 0.4 + 0.4 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.6)),  // global
];
```

Each cycle, pick 2 consecutive patterns for the 2 heads.

Now for the sweep during attention phase, I want to sweep through heads:
```typescript
// During attention: X sweeps 0→1, and heads sweep 0→N_HEADS-1
if (phase === "attn") {
  sweepPos = phaseT; // 0→1
  headSweep = phaseT * N_HEADS; // 0→2
} else if (phase === "ffn") {
  sweepPos = 1 + phaseT * 2; // 1→3
  headSweep = -1;
} else {
  sweepPos = 3;
  headSweep = -1;
}
```

For edge highlighting during attention:
- An attn edge to head h is highlighted when:
  1. The X sweep is between stage 0 and stage 1 (always true during attn phase)
  2. The head sweep is near h

```typescript
if (e.kind === "attn" && headSweep >= 0) {
  const headProx = Math.exp(-((headSweep - e.headIdx - 0.5) ** 2) / 0.5);
  alpha += headProx * 0.15;
}
```

For node activation during attention:
- Stage 0 nodes: activated at the start of attention

```typescript
for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {

        const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 0);
        const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3);
        n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, bump);
      } else if (s === 1) {

        const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 1);
        const xBump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3);
        const headDist = Math.abs(headSweep - n.zIdx - 0.5);
        const headBump = Math.exp(-(headDist * headDist) / 0.4);
        n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, xBump * headBump);

        n.act *= 0.84;

    } else if (phase === "ffn") {
      const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
      const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.5);
      n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, bump);
    } else {
      n.act *= 0.8;
    }
  }
}
```

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  yIdx: number;
  zIdx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  twinkle: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "attn" | "ffn" | "skip";
  headIdx: number;
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}

// 3D Transformer block: input → multi-head attention → FFN → output
//   stage 0: input tokens         (4 positions × 1 = 4 nodes)
//   stage 1: multi-head attention  (4 positions × 2 heads = 8 nodes, heads stacked in Z)
//   stage 2: FFN hidden            (4 positions × 2 Z-layers = 8 nodes)
//   stage 3: output               (4 positions × 1 = 4 nodes)
const STAGE_Y = [4, 4, 4, 4];
const STAGE_Z = [1, 2, 2, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = 4;
const N_TOK = STAGE_Y[0];
const N_HEADS = STAGE_Z[1];

// Per-head attention patterns — each head attends differently.
const PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.4),    // local window
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.28 + 0.12 * ((i + j) % 2)), // mixed diagonal
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0), // shifted peak
  (i, j) => (j >= i ? Math.exp(-(j - i) / 2.0) : 0.15), // causal-forward
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j + 1) ** 2) / 2.0), // shifted other way
  (i, j) => 0.35 + 0.45 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.6)), // global
];

// Single muted slate-blue family.
const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  skip: "hsl(205, 16%, 44%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 30%, 64%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
};
const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

const CAM_TILT = -0.35;
const FOCAL = 900;
const CAM_Z = 760;

export default function ParticleField() {
  // ... (same structure as before but with 3D node layout)
}
```

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  yIdx: number;
  zIdx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  twinkle: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "attn" | "ffn" | "skip";
  headIdx: number;
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}

// 3D Transformer block. Nodes are arranged in a genuine 3D volume:
//   X axis: processing stage (input → attention → FFN → output)
//   Y axis: sequence position (token index)
//   Z axis: attention head / feature depth
//
//   stage 0: input tokens          (5 positions × 1        = 5 nodes)
//   stage 1: multi-head attention  (5 positions × 2 heads  = 10 nodes, heads in Z)
//   stage 2: FFN hidden            (5 positions × 2 layers = 10 nodes)
//   stage 3: output                (5 positions × 1        = 5 nodes)
const STAGE_Y = [5, 5, 5, 5];
const STAGE_Z = [1, 2, 2, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = 4;
const N_TOK = STAGE_Y[0];
const N_HEADS = STAGE_Z[1];

const PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.4),
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.28 + 0.12 * ((i + j) % 2)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
  (i, j) => (j >= i ? Math.exp(-(j - i) / 2.0) : 0.15),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j + 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
  (i, j) => 0.35 + 0.45 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.6)),
];

const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  skip: "hsl(205, 16%, 44%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 30%, 64%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
};
const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

const CAM_TILT = -0.35;
const FOCAL = 900;
const CAM_Z = 760;

export default function ParticleField() {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
    if (!canvas) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d", { alpha: true });
    if (!ctx) return;

    let animationId = 0;
    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let dpr = 1;
    let resizeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

    let nodes: NetNode[][] = [];
    let edges: NetEdge[] = [];
    let posPoints: Array<{ x: number; y: number; z: number }> = [];
    let runStart = performance.now();
    let nextReset = 30 + Math.random() * 14;
    let cycle = 0;
    let patOffset = Math.floor(Math.random() * PATTERNS.length);

    const ATTN_DUR = 3.2;
    const FFN_DUR = 3.2;
    const SETTLE_DUR = 1.4;
    const CYCLE_DUR = ATTN_DUR + FFN_DUR + SETTLE_DUR;

    const randn = () =>
      (Math.random() + Math.random() + Math.random() - 1.5) * 0.5;

    // Layout constants (set on resize).
    let stageSpacingX = 140;
    let nodeSpacingY = 22;
    let headSpacingZ = 60;
    let layoutCy = 0;

    const getNode = (s: number, yi: number, zi: number): NetNode =>
      nodes[s][zi * STAGE_Y[s] + yi];

    const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number, rotY: number) => {
      const cy = Math.cos(rotY);
      const sy = Math.sin(rotY);
      const rx = wx * cy - wz * sy;
      const rz = wx * sy + wz * cy;
      const ct = Math.cos(CAM_TILT);
      const st = Math.sin(CAM_TILT);
      const ty = wy * ct - rz * st;
      const tz = wy * st + rz * ct;
      const denom = CAM_Z + tz;
      const safeDenom = denom > 1 ? denom : 1;
      const scale = FOCAL / safeDenom;
      return {
        sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
        sy: layoutCy + ty * scale,
        depth: tz,
        scale,
      };
    };

    const buildNetwork = () => {
      nodes = [];
      edges = [];
      posPoints = [];

      const availW = width - 280;
      stageSpacingX = Math.max(120, Math.min(170, availW / (NUM_STAGES - 1)));
      const maxNodesY = Math.max(...STAGE_Y);
      const availH = height * 0.78;
      nodeSpacingY = Math.min(20, availH / (maxNodesY - 1));
      headSpacingZ = Math.min(70, stageSpacingX * 0.45);
      layoutCy = height * 0.5;

      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const yCount = STAGE_Y[s];
        const zCount = STAGE_Z[s];
        const stageNodes: NetNode[] = [];
        const wx = (s - (NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2) * stageSpacingX;
        for (let zi = 0; zi < zCount; zi++) {
          for (let yi = 0; yi < yCount; yi++) {
            stageNodes.push({
              stage: s,
              yIdx: yi,
              zIdx: zi,
              x: wx,
              y: (yi - (yCount - 1) / 2) * nodeSpacingY,
              z: (zi - (zCount - 1) / 2) * headSpacingZ,
              act: 0,
              twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            });
          }
        }
        nodes.push(stageNodes);
      }

      // Attention edges: stage 0 → stage 1 (per-head patterns)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        for (let yj = 0; yj < N_TOK; yj++) {
          for (let h = 0; h < N_HEADS; h++) {
            const a = getNode(0, yi, 0);
            const b = getNode(1, yj, h);
            const pat = PATTERNS[(patOffset + h) % PATTERNS.length];
            const w = pat(yi, yj);
            const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
            const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
            const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + (b.x - a.x) * 0.04;
            edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "attn", headIdx: h, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
          }
        }
      }

      // FFN1: stage 1 → stage 2 (all-to-all)
      for (const a of nodes[1]) {
        for (const b of nodes[2]) {
          const w = randn() * 0.35;
          const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
          const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
          const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2;
          edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "ffn", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
        }
      }

      // FFN2: stage 2 → stage 3 (all-to-all)
      for (const a of nodes[2]) {
        for (const b of nodes[3]) {
          const w = randn() * 0.35;
          const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
          const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
          const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2;
          edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "ffn", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
        }
      }

      // Residual A: stage 0 → stage 2 (same yIdx, zIdx=0)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        const a = getNode(0, yi, 0);
        const b = getNode(2, yi, 0);
        const w = randn() * 0.25;
        const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
        const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
        const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + 80; // arc out in +Z
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "skip", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
      }

      // Residual B: stage 2 → stage 3 (same yIdx, zIdx=0)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        const a = getNode(2, yi, 0);
        const b = getNode(3, yi, 0);
        const w = randn() * 0.25;
        const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
        const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
        const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + 80;
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "skip", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
      }

      // Positional encoding: 3D helix around the input token column
      const tokX = nodes[0][0].x;
      const tokZ = nodes[0][0].z;
      const tokY0 = nodes[0][0].y;
      const tokY1 = nodes[0][N_TOK - 1].y;
      const span = tokY1 - tokY0;
      for (let i = 0; i < N_TOK; i++) {
        const t = i / (N_TOK - 1);
        const ang = t * TAU * 0.9;
        posPoints.push({
          x: tokX - 32 + Math.sin(ang) * 12,
          y: tokY0 + t * span,
          z: tokZ + Math.cos(ang) * 16,
        });
      }
    };

    const resetRun = () => {
      patOffset = (patOffset + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (PATTERNS.length - 1))) % PATTERNS.length;
      for (const e of edges) {
        if (e.kind === "attn") {
          const pat = PATTERNS[(patOffset + e.headIdx) % PATTERNS.length];
          e.tw = pat(e.a.yIdx, e.b.yIdx);
        } else {
          e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * (e.kind === "skip" ? 0.04 : 0.05);
        }
      }
      runStart = performance.now();
      nextReset = 30 + Math.random() * 14;
      cycle = 0;
    };

    const resize = () => {
      dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2);
      width = window.innerWidth;
      height = Math.min(360, Math.max(240, Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.36)));
      canvas.width = Math.floor(width * dpr);
      canvas.height = Math.floor(height * dpr);
      canvas.style.width = `${width}px`;
      canvas.style.height = `${height}px`;
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
      buildNetwork();
    };

    const debouncedResize = () => {
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      resizeTimer = setTimeout(resize, 180);
    };

    const fillCircle = (sx: number, sy: number, r: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (r < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(sx, sy, r, 0, TAU);
      ctx.fill();
    };

    const draw = (now: number) => {
      const rt = (now - runStart) * 0.001;
      if (rt > nextReset) resetRun();

      const cycleTime = rt % CYCLE_DUR;
      let phase: "attn" | "ffn" | "settle" = "attn";
      let phaseT = 0;
      if (cycleTime < ATTN_DUR) {
        phase = "attn";
        phaseT = cycleTime / ATTN_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < ATTN_DUR + FFN_DUR) {
        phase = "ffn";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - ATTN_DUR) / FFN_DUR;
      } else {
        phase = "settle";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - ATTN_DUR - FFN_DUR) / SETTLE_DUR;
      }

      const curCycle = Math.floor(rt / CYCLE_DUR);
      if (curCycle !== cycle) {
        cycle = curCycle;
        for (const e of edges) {
          if (e.kind !== "attn") e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.03;
        }
        for (const n of nodes[0]) n.twinkle = Math.random() * TAU;
      }

      const updateRate = phase === "settle" ? 0.06 : 0.012;
      for (const e of edges) e.w += (e.tw - e.w) * updateRate;

      // Sweep positions
      let sweepPos = 0;
      let headSweep = -1;
      if (phase === "attn") {
        sweepPos = phaseT; // 0 → 1
        headSweep = phaseT * N_HEADS; // 0 → N_HEADS
      } else if (phase === "ffn") {
        sweepPos = 1 + phaseT * 2; // 1 → 3
      } else {
        sweepPos = 3;
      }

      // Node activation
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          if (phase === "settle") {
            n.act *= 0.8;
          } else if (phase === "attn") {
            if (s === 0) {
              const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 0);
              n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3));
            } else if (s === 1) {
              const xDist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 1);
              const xBump = Math.exp(-(xDist * xDist) / 0.3);
              const hDist = Math.abs(headSweep - n.zIdx - 0.5);
              const hBump = Math.exp(-(hDist * hDist) / 0.4);
              n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, xBump * hBump);
            } else {
              n.act *= 0.84;
            }
          } else {
            // ffn
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.5));
          }
        }
      }

      // fade
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4)";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);

      const rotY = rt * 0.15;
      const isSweeping = phase === "attn" || phase === "ffn";

      // Project all nodes
      const projNodes: Array<{ n: NetNode; sx: number; sy: number; depth: number; scale: number }> = [];
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          const p = project(n.x, n.y, n.z, rotY);
          projNodes.push({ n, sx: p.sx, sy: p.sy, depth: p.depth, scale: p.scale });
        }
      }

      // Positional encoding helix
      if (posPoints.length > 0) {
        ctx.globalAlpha = 0.12;
        ctx.strokeStyle = C.pos;
        ctx.lineWidth = 0.7;
        ctx.beginPath();
        for (let i = 0; i < posPoints.length; i++) {
          const p = project(posPoints[i].x, posPoints[i].y, posPoints[i].z, rotY);
          if (i === 0) ctx.moveTo(p.sx, p.sy);
          else ctx.lineTo(p.sx, p.sy);
        }
        ctx.stroke();
        for (const pp of posPoints) {
          const p = project(pp.x, pp.y, pp.z, rotY);
          fillCircle(p.sx, p.sy, 1.0 * p.scale, C.pos, 0.16);
        }
      }

      // Draw edges back-to-front
      type DrawItem = { depth: number; fn: () => void };
      const drawItems: DrawItem[] = [];

      for (const e of edges) {
        const a = e.a;
        const b = e.b;
        const lo = a.stage;
        const hi = b.stage;
        const mag = Math.abs(e.w);
        const sweeping = isSweeping && sweepPos >= lo - 0.1 && sweepPos <= hi + 0.1;

        let alpha = 0.02 + mag * 0.07;
        if (sweeping) {
          const span = hi - lo;
          const edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
          const prox = Math.sin(
            Math.min(1, Math.max(0, 1 - Math.abs(edgeProgress - 0.5) * 2)) * Math.PI * 0.5,
          );
          alpha += prox * 0.12;
        }

        // Head spotlight during attention
        if (e.kind === "attn" && headSweep >= 0) {
          const hProx = Math.exp(-((headSweep - e.headIdx - 0.5) ** 2) / 0.4);
          alpha += hProx * 0.1;
        }

        const pa = project(a.x, a.y, a.z, rotY);
        const pb = project(b.x, b.y, b.z, rotY);
        const avgDepth = (pa.depth + pb.depth) * 0.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, avgDepth / 600)) * 0.5;
        const finalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha * depthFactor);
        const lw = (0.35 + mag * 0.7) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.6, (pa.scale + pb.scale) * 0.5));

        if (e.kind === "skip") {
          const pc = project(e.ctrlX, e.ctrlY, e.ctrlZ, rotY);
          drawItems.push({
            depth: avgDepth,
            fn: () => {
              ctx.setLineDash(DASH);
              ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
              ctx.strokeStyle = C.skip;
              ctx.lineWidth = lw;
              ctx.beginPath();
              ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
              ctx.quadraticCurveTo(pc.sx, pc.sy, pb.sx, pb.sy);
              ctx.stroke();
            },
          });
        } else {
          drawItems.push({
            depth: avgDepth,
            fn: () => {
              ctx.setLineDash([]);
              ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
              ctx.strokeStyle = C.edge;
              ctx.lineWidth = lw;
              ctx.beginPath();
              ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
              ctx.lineTo(pb.sx, pb.sy);
              ctx.stroke();

              if (sweeping && mag > 0.25) {
                const span = hi - lo;
                const edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
                const p = edgeProgress;
                fillCircle(
                  pa.sx + (pb.sx - pa.sx) * p,
                  pa.sy + (pb.sy - pa.sy) * p,
                  1.1 * pa.scale,
                  C.pulse,
                  0.35,
                );
              }
            },
          });
        }
      }

      drawItems.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const it of drawItems) it.fn();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);

      // Nodes back-to-front
      projNodes.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const { n, sx, sy, scale } of projNodes) {
        const isEdge = n.stage === 0 || n.stage === NUM_STAGES - 1;
        const baseR = (isEdge ? 2.8 : 2.2) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.6, scale));
        const inputBoost = n.stage === 0 ? 0.18 + 0.12 * Math.sin(rt * 0.8 + n.twinkle) : 0;
        const glow = Math.max(n.act, Math.max(0, inputBoost));
        const r = baseR + glow * 1.2;

        if (glow > 0.05) {
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 2.4, C.node, glow * 0.07);
        }
        fillCircle(sx, sy, r, C.node, 0.32 + glow * 0.2);
      }

      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };

    resize();
    resetRun();
    window.addEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
    animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
    };
  }, []);

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      aria-hidden="true"
     className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-0 block"
    />
  );
}
```

```typescript
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.3 + 0.1 * ((i + j) % 2)),  // head 1: mixed
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),  // shifted
```

```typescript
// During attention: X sweeps 0→1, and heads sweep 0→N_HEADS-1
if (phase === "attn") {
  sweepPos = phaseT; // 0→1
  headSweep = phaseT * N_HEADS; // 0→2
} else if (phase === "ffn") {
  sweepPos = 1 + phaseT * 2; // 1→3
  headSweep = -1;
} else {
  sweepPos = 3;
  headSweep = -1;
}
```

For edge highlighting during attention:
- An attn edge to head h is highlighted when:
  1. The X sweep is between stage 0 and stage 1 (always true during attn phase)
  2. The head sweep is near h

```typescript

  const headProx = Math.exp(-((headSweep - e.headIdx - 0.5) ** 2) / 0.5);
  alpha += headProx * 0.15;
}
```

For node activation during attention:
- Stage 0 nodes: activated at the start of attention
- Stage 1 nodes: activated based on head sweep (each head's nodes light up when headSweep is near)

```typescript
for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
  for (const n of nodes[s]) {
    if (phase === "attn") {
      if (s === 0) {
        const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 0);
        const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3);
        n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, bump);
      } else if (s === 1) {
        // light up based on head sweep
        const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 1);
        const xBump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3);
        const headDist = Math.abs(headSweep - n.zIdx - 0.5);
        const headBump = Math.exp(-(headDist * headDist) / 0.4);
        n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, xBump * headBump);
      } else {
        n.act *= 0.84;
      }
    } else if (phase === "ffn") {
      const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
      const bump = Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.5);
      n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, bump);
    } else {
      n.act *= 0.8;
    }
  }
}
```

```typescript
import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  yIdx: number;
  zIdx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  twinkle: number;
}

  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "attn" | "ffn" | "skip";
  headIdx: number;
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;

//   stage 0: input tokens         (4 positions × 1 = 4 nodes)
//   stage 1: multi-head attention  (4 positions × 2 heads = 8 nodes, heads stacked in Z)
//   stage 2: FFN hidden            (4 positions × 2 Z-layers = 8 nodes)
//   stage 3: output               (4 positions × 1 = 4 nodes)

const STAGE_Z = [1, 2, 2, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = 4;
const N_TOK = STAGE_Y[0];
const N_HEADS = STAGE_Z[1];

// Per-head attention patterns — each head attends differently.
const PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.4),    // local window
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.28 + 0.12 * ((i + j) % 2)), // mixed diagonal
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0), // shifted peak
  (i, j) => (j >= i ? Math.exp(-(j - i) / 2.0) : 0.15), // causal-forward
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j + 1) ** 2) / 2.0), // shifted other way
  (i, j) => 0.35 + 0.45 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.6)), // global
];

// Single muted slate-blue family.
const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  skip: "hsl(205, 16%, 44%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 30%, 64%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
};
const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

const CAM_TILT = -0.35;
const FOCAL = 900;
const CAM_Z = 760;

export default function ParticleField() {
  // ... (same structure as before but with 3D node layout)
}
```

```typescript
"use client";

import { useEffect, useRef } from "react";

interface NetNode {
  stage: number;
  yIdx: number;
  zIdx: number;
  x: number;
  y: number;
  z: number;
  act: number;
  twinkle: number;
}

interface NetEdge {
  a: NetNode;
  b: NetNode;
  w: number;
  tw: number;
  kind: "attn" | "ffn" | "skip";
  headIdx: number;
  ctrlX: number;
  ctrlY: number;
  ctrlZ: number;
}

// 3D Transformer block. Nodes are arranged in a genuine 3D volume:
//   X axis: processing stage (input → attention → FFN → output)
//   Y axis: sequence position (token index)
//   Z axis: attention head / feature depth
//
//   stage 0: input tokens          (5 positions × 1        = 5 nodes)
//   stage 1: multi-head attention  (5 positions × 2 heads  = 10 nodes, heads in Z)
//   stage 2: FFN hidden            (5 positions × 2 layers = 10 nodes)
//   stage 3: output                (5 positions × 1        = 5 nodes)
const STAGE_Y = [5, 5, 5, 5];
const STAGE_Z = [1, 2, 2, 1];
const NUM_STAGES = 4;
const N_TOK = STAGE_Y[0];
const N_HEADS = STAGE_Z[1];

const PATTERNS: Array<(i: number, j: number) => number> = [
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-Math.abs(i - j) / 1.4),
  (i, j) => (i === j ? 0.9 : 0.28 + 0.12 * ((i + j) % 2)),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j - 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
  (i, j) => (j >= i ? Math.exp(-(j - i) / 2.0) : 0.15),
  (i, j) => Math.exp(-((i - j + 1) ** 2) / 2.0),
  (i, j) => 0.35 + 0.45 * Math.abs(Math.sin((i + 1) * (j + 1) * 0.6)),
];

const C = {
  edge: "hsl(205, 22%, 52%)",
  skip: "hsl(205, 16%, 44%)",
  pulse: "hsl(200, 30%, 64%)",
  node: "hsl(205, 24%, 58%)",
  pos: "hsl(205, 20%, 50%)",
};
const DASH = [3, 4];
const TAU = Math.PI * 2;

const CAM_TILT = -0.35;
const FOCAL = 900;
const CAM_Z = 760;

export default function ParticleField() {
  const canvasRef = useRef<HTMLCanvasElement>(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    const canvas = canvasRef.current;
    if (!canvas) return;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d", { alpha: true });
    if (!ctx) return;

    let animationId = 0;
    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let dpr = 1;
    let resizeTimer: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null = null;

    let nodes: NetNode[][] = [];
    let edges: NetEdge[] = [];
    let posPoints: Array<{ x: number; y: number; z: number }> = [];
    let runStart = performance.now();
    let nextReset = 30 + Math.random() * 14;
    let cycle = 0;
    let patOffset = Math.floor(Math.random() * PATTERNS.length);

    const ATTN_DUR = 3.2;
    const FFN_DUR = 3.2;
    const SETTLE_DUR = 1.4;
    const CYCLE_DUR = ATTN_DUR + FFN_DUR + SETTLE_DUR;

    const randn = () =>
      (Math.random() + Math.random() + Math.random() - 1.5) * 0.5;

    // Layout constants (set on resize).
    let stageSpacingX = 140;
    let nodeSpacingY = 22;
    let headSpacingZ = 60;
    let layoutCy = 0;

    const getNode = (s: number, yi: number, zi: number): NetNode =>
      nodes[s][zi * STAGE_Y[s] + yi];

    const project = (wx: number, wy: number, wz: number, rotY: number) => {
      const cy = Math.cos(rotY);
      const sy = Math.sin(rotY);
      const rx = wx * cy - wz * sy;
      const rz = wx * sy + wz * cy;
      const ct = Math.cos(CAM_TILT);
      const st = Math.sin(CAM_TILT);
      const ty = wy * ct - rz * st;
      const tz = wy * st + rz * ct;
      const denom = CAM_Z + tz;
      const safeDenom = denom > 1 ? denom : 1;
      const scale = FOCAL / safeDenom;
      return {
        sx: width / 2 + rx * scale,
        sy: layoutCy + ty * scale,
        depth: tz,
        scale,
      };
    };

    const buildNetwork = () => {
      nodes = [];
      edges = [];
      posPoints = [];

      const availW = width - 280;
      stageSpacingX = Math.max(120, Math.min(170, availW / (NUM_STAGES - 1)));
      const maxNodesY = Math.max(...STAGE_Y);
      const availH = height * 0.78;
      nodeSpacingY = Math.min(20, availH / (maxNodesY - 1));
      headSpacingZ = Math.min(70, stageSpacingX * 0.45);
      layoutCy = height * 0.5;

      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        const yCount = STAGE_Y[s];
        const zCount = STAGE_Z[s];
        const stageNodes: NetNode[] = [];
        const wx = (s - (NUM_STAGES - 1) / 2) * stageSpacingX;
        for (let zi = 0; zi < zCount; zi++) {
          for (let yi = 0; yi < yCount; yi++) {
            stageNodes.push({
              stage: s,
              yIdx: yi,
              zIdx: zi,
              x: wx,
              y: (yi - (yCount - 1) / 2) * nodeSpacingY,
              z: (zi - (zCount - 1) / 2) * headSpacingZ,
              act: 0,
              twinkle: Math.random() * TAU,
            });
          }
        }
        nodes.push(stageNodes);
      }

      // Attention edges: stage 0 → stage 1 (per-head patterns)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        for (let yj = 0; yj < N_TOK; yj++) {
          for (let h = 0; h < N_HEADS; h++) {
            const a = getNode(0, yi, 0);
            const b = getNode(1, yj, h);
            const pat = PATTERNS[(patOffset + h) % PATTERNS.length];
            const w = pat(yi, yj);
            const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
            const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
            const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + (b.x - a.x) * 0.04;
            edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "attn", headIdx: h, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
          }
        }
      }

      // FFN1: stage 1 → stage 2 (all-to-all)
      for (const a of nodes[1]) {
        for (const b of nodes[2]) {
          const w = randn() * 0.35;
          const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
          const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
          const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2;
          edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "ffn", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
        }
      }

      // FFN2: stage 2 → stage 3 (all-to-all)
      for (const a of nodes[2]) {
        for (const b of nodes[3]) {
          const w = randn() * 0.35;
          const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
          const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
          const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2;
          edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "ffn", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
        }
      }

      // Residual A: stage 0 → stage 2 (same yIdx, zIdx=0)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        const a = getNode(0, yi, 0);
        const b = getNode(2, yi, 0);
        const w = randn() * 0.25;
        const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
        const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
        const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + 80; // arc out in +Z
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "skip", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
      }

      // Residual B: stage 2 → stage 3 (same yIdx, zIdx=0)
      for (let yi = 0; yi < N_TOK; yi++) {
        const a = getNode(2, yi, 0);
        const b = getNode(3, yi, 0);
        const w = randn() * 0.25;
        const cx = (a.x + b.x) / 2;
        const cy = (a.y + b.y) / 2;
        const cz = (a.z + b.z) / 2 + 80;
        edges.push({ a, b, w, tw: w, kind: "skip", headIdx: -1, ctrlX: cx, ctrlY: cy, ctrlZ: cz });
      }

      // Positional encoding: 3D helix around the input token column
      const tokX = nodes[0][0].x;
      const tokZ = nodes[0][0].z;
      const tokY0 = nodes[0][0].y;
      const tokY1 = nodes[0][N_TOK - 1].y;
      const span = tokY1 - tokY0;
      for (let i = 0; i < N_TOK; i++) {
        const t = i / (N_TOK - 1);
        const ang = t * TAU * 0.9;
        posPoints.push({
          x: tokX - 32 + Math.sin(ang) * 12,
          y: tokY0 + t * span,
          z: tokZ + Math.cos(ang) * 16,
        });
      }
    };

    const resetRun = () => {
      patOffset = (patOffset + 1 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (PATTERNS.length - 1))) % PATTERNS.length;
      for (const e of edges) {
        if (e.kind === "attn") {
          const pat = PATTERNS[(patOffset + e.headIdx) % PATTERNS.length];
          e.tw = pat(e.a.yIdx, e.b.yIdx);
        } else {
          e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * (e.kind === "skip" ? 0.04 : 0.05);
        }
      }
      runStart = performance.now();
      nextReset = 30 + Math.random() * 14;
      cycle = 0;
    };

    const resize = () => {
      dpr = Math.min(window.devicePixelRatio || 1, 2);
      width = window.innerWidth;
      height = Math.min(360, Math.max(240, Math.floor(window.innerHeight * 0.36)));
      canvas.width = Math.floor(width * dpr);
      canvas.height = Math.floor(height * dpr);
      canvas.style.width = `${width}px`;
      canvas.style.height = `${height}px`;
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#000000";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);
      buildNetwork();
    };

    const debouncedResize = () => {
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      resizeTimer = setTimeout(resize, 180);
    };

    const fillCircle = (sx: number, sy: number, r: number, color: string, alpha: number) => {
      if (r < 0.2) return;
      ctx.globalAlpha = alpha;
      ctx.fillStyle = color;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.arc(sx, sy, r, 0, TAU);
      ctx.fill();
    };

    const draw = (now: number) => {
      const rt = (now - runStart) * 0.001;
      if (rt > nextReset) resetRun();

      const cycleTime = rt % CYCLE_DUR;
      let phase: "attn" | "ffn" | "settle" = "attn";
      let phaseT = 0;
      if (cycleTime < ATTN_DUR) {
        phase = "attn";
        phaseT = cycleTime / ATTN_DUR;
      } else if (cycleTime < ATTN_DUR + FFN_DUR) {
        phase = "ffn";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - ATTN_DUR) / FFN_DUR;
      } else {
        phase = "settle";
        phaseT = (cycleTime - ATTN_DUR - FFN_DUR) / SETTLE_DUR;
      }

      const curCycle = Math.floor(rt / CYCLE_DUR);
      if (curCycle !== cycle) {
        cycle = curCycle;
        for (const e of edges) {
          if (e.kind !== "attn") e.tw += (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.03;
        }
        for (const n of nodes[0]) n.twinkle = Math.random() * TAU;
      }

      const updateRate = phase === "settle" ? 0.06 : 0.012;
      for (const e of edges) e.w += (e.tw - e.w) * updateRate;

      // Sweep positions
      let sweepPos = 0;
      let headSweep = -1;
      if (phase === "attn") {
        sweepPos = phaseT; // 0 → 1
        headSweep = phaseT * N_HEADS; // 0 → N_HEADS
      } else if (phase === "ffn") {
        sweepPos = 1 + phaseT * 2; // 1 → 3
      } else {
        sweepPos = 3;
      }

      // Node activation
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          if (phase === "settle") {
            n.act *= 0.8;
          } else if (phase === "attn") {
            if (s === 0) {
              const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 0);
              n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.3));
            } else if (s === 1) {
              const xDist = Math.abs(sweepPos - 1);
              const xBump = Math.exp(-(xDist * xDist) / 0.3);
              const hDist = Math.abs(headSweep - n.zIdx - 0.5);
              const hBump = Math.exp(-(hDist * hDist) / 0.4);
              n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, xBump * hBump);
            } else {
              n.act *= 0.84;
            }
          } else {
            // ffn
            const dist = Math.abs(sweepPos - s);
            n.act = Math.max(n.act * 0.84, Math.exp(-(dist * dist) / 0.5));
          }
        }
      }

      // fade
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4)";
      ctx.fillRect(0, 0, width, height);

      const rotY = rt * 0.15;
      const isSweeping = phase === "attn" || phase === "ffn";

      // Project all nodes
      const projNodes: Array<{ n: NetNode; sx: number; sy: number; depth: number; scale: number }> = [];
      for (let s = 0; s < NUM_STAGES; s++) {
        for (const n of nodes[s]) {
          const p = project(n.x, n.y, n.z, rotY);
          projNodes.push({ n, sx: p.sx, sy: p.sy, depth: p.depth, scale: p.scale });
        }
      }

      // Positional encoding helix
      if (posPoints.length > 0) {
        ctx.globalAlpha = 0.12;
        ctx.strokeStyle = C.pos;
        ctx.lineWidth = 0.7;
        ctx.beginPath();
        for (let i = 0; i < posPoints.length; i++) {
          const p = project(posPoints[i].x, posPoints[i].y, posPoints[i].z, rotY);
          if (i === 0) ctx.moveTo(p.sx, p.sy);
          else ctx.lineTo(p.sx, p.sy);
        }
        ctx.stroke();
        for (const pp of posPoints) {
          const p = project(pp.x, pp.y, pp.z, rotY);
          fillCircle(p.sx, p.sy, 1.0 * p.scale, C.pos, 0.16);
        }
      }

      // Draw edges back-to-front
      type DrawItem = { depth: number; fn: () => void };
      const drawItems: DrawItem[] = [];

      for (const e of edges) {
        const a = e.a;
        const b = e.b;
        const lo = a.stage;
        const hi = b.stage;
        const mag = Math.abs(e.w);
        const sweeping = isSweeping && sweepPos >= lo - 0.1 && sweepPos <= hi + 0.1;

        let alpha = 0.02 + mag * 0.07;
        if (sweeping) {
          const span = hi - lo;
          const edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
          const prox = Math.sin(
            Math.min(1, Math.max(0, 1 - Math.abs(edgeProgress - 0.5) * 2)) * Math.PI * 0.5,
          );
          alpha += prox * 0.12;
        }

        // Head spotlight during attention
        if (e.kind === "attn" && headSweep >= 0) {
          const hProx = Math.exp(-((headSweep - e.headIdx - 0.5) ** 2) / 0.4);
          alpha += hProx * 0.1;
        }

        const pa = project(a.x, a.y, a.z, rotY);
        const pb = project(b.x, b.y, b.z, rotY);
        const avgDepth = (pa.depth + pb.depth) * 0.5;
        const depthFactor = 1 - Math.min(1, Math.max(0, avgDepth / 600)) * 0.5;
        const finalAlpha = Math.min(1, alpha * depthFactor);
        const lw = (0.35 + mag * 0.7) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.6, (pa.scale + pb.scale) * 0.5));

        if (e.kind === "skip") {
          const pc = project(e.ctrlX, e.ctrlY, e.ctrlZ, rotY);
          drawItems.push({
            depth: avgDepth,
            fn: () => {
              ctx.setLineDash(DASH);
              ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
              ctx.strokeStyle = C.skip;
              ctx.lineWidth = lw;
              ctx.beginPath();
              ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
              ctx.quadraticCurveTo(pc.sx, pc.sy, pb.sx, pb.sy);
              ctx.stroke();
            },
          });
        } else {
          drawItems.push({
            depth: avgDepth,
            fn: () => {
              ctx.setLineDash([]);
              ctx.globalAlpha = finalAlpha;
              ctx.strokeStyle = C.edge;
              ctx.lineWidth = lw;
              ctx.beginPath();
              ctx.moveTo(pa.sx, pa.sy);
              ctx.lineTo(pb.sx, pb.sy);
              ctx.stroke();

              if (sweeping && mag > 0.25) {
                const span = hi - lo;
                const edgeProgress = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (sweepPos - lo) / span));
                const p = edgeProgress;
                fillCircle(
                  pa.sx + (pb.sx - pa.sx) * p,
                  pa.sy + (pb.sy - pa.sy) * p,
                  1.1 * pa.scale,
                  C.pulse,
                  0.35,
                );
              }
            },
          });
        }
      }

      drawItems.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const it of drawItems) it.fn();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);

      // Nodes back-to-front
      projNodes.sort((x, y) => y.depth - x.depth);
      for (const { n, sx, sy, scale } of projNodes) {
        const isEdge = n.stage === 0 || n.stage === NUM_STAGES - 1;
        const baseR = (isEdge ? 2.8 : 2.2) * Math.min(1.3, Math.max(0.6, scale));
        const inputBoost = n.stage === 0 ? 0.18 + 0.12 * Math.sin(rt * 0.8 + n.twinkle) : 0;
        const glow = Math.max(n.act, Math.max(0, inputBoost));
        const r = baseR + glow * 1.2;

        if (glow > 0.05) {
          fillCircle(sx, sy, r * 2.4, C.node, glow * 0.07);
        }
        fillCircle(sx, sy, r, C.node, 0.32 + glow * 0.2);
      }

      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);
    };

    resize();
    resetRun();
    window.addEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
    animationId = requestAnimationFrame(draw);

    return () => {
      window.removeEventListener("resize", debouncedResize);
      if (resizeTimer) clearTimeout(resizeTimer);
      cancelAnimationFrame(animationId);
    };
  }, []);

  return (
    <canvas
      ref={canvasRef}
      aria-hidden="true"
     className="pointer-events-none fixed inset-x-0 top-0 z-0 block"
    />
  );
}
```

content.text:
ネットワーク自体を3D構造にしました。tscとeslint両方クリア済みです。

変更点：
- **ノードを3Dリング配置**: 各ステージのノードが平面的な縦1列から、Y-Z平面のリング（円環）に配置。6ノードステージは1つのリング、12ノードのFFN隠れ層は2段に重ねたリング（上下にオフセット）で立体的に
- **エッジの3D接続**: リング状のノード間を3D空間で結ぶ直線。カメラ回転でリングの表裏が見える
- **skip接続**: 制御点をリングの半径方向に外側へ膨らませて3Dベジェ曲線に
- **positional encoding**: トークンリングを取り囲む3Dヘリックスに変更
- **カメラ**: Y軸回転で3D構造全体がゆっくり回転し、リングの奥行きや重なりが立体的に見える
